commit.message:
Exempt FloodAdvert/FloodDemand from global flood dedup

These are peer-specific pull-control messages that must be delivered
per-peer. Stellar-core handles them directly in Peer::recvFloodAdvert
and Peer::recvFloodDemand, not through Floodgate.

Closes #1182

fix: Caddy handle ordering — API proxy before SPA fallback

The try_files directive was catching /api/* requests and rewriting
them to index.html before the reverse_proxy could handle them.
Fixed by wrapping each concern in separate handle blocks so
/api/* is matched first.

Cache ledger close times in getEvents to eliminate N+1 queries

Each event previously triggered a separate load_ledger_header() call
that decoded full XDR. Now close times are cached per ledger_seq in a
HashMap within the response loop.

Closes #1157

commit.message:
Reserve pending peer_id during outbound handshake to prevent multi-address exhaustion

Outbound discovery connections to different addresses from the same
peer_id could both remain pending after HELLO revealed the same identity.
Now peer_id is reserved in PendingConnections.by_peer_id during the
handshake (after recv_hello) for both inbound and outbound connections.

Matches stellar-core Peer::recvHello() which scans pending and
authenticated peers for duplicate node IDs before continuing.

Closes #1217

commit.message:
fix: replace 1000-block sled scan with in-memory reward counter to prevent RPC stall

The watchdog was restarting nodes because get_pool_reward_tracking() was
scanning up to 1000 blocks from sled synchronously on the async runtime
thread — blocking the RPC accept loop for 1-3+ seconds per block.

Changes:
- Add update_reward_counters(): O(n) in-memory counter update called from
  add_block; persists rewarded nodes immediately and all nodes every 10
  blocks via sled_insert_bg (non-blocking background writer)
- Add get_reward_tracking_from_memory(): O(n) read-lock, replaces the
  1000-block sled scan in produce_block_at_height, validate_pool_distribution,
  and the main.rs block-production loop
- Add reconstruct_reward_counters(): O(n) startup recovery from persisted
  last_reward_height, called after blockchain init so counters are accurate
  from block 1 without any sled scan

This eliminates the RPC stall that was causing busy_streak to escalate
to fail_streak and trigger watchdog restarts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

at runtime.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Reject HELLO with listening_port 0 to prevent peer gossip poisoning

Accepting port 0 in HELLO caused the inbound peer's TCP source port to
be stored as its listening address, which could be gossiped to other
peers as a reachable endpoint.

Fix: reject listening_port <= 0 in process_hello(), matching
stellar-core Peer::recvHello() which rejects listeningPort <= 0.

Closes #1215

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: use correct NES API endpoints for usage data

Updated to match the actual NES Angular app flow:
- /rest/account/customer/ → basic account info
- /rest/account/summary → paymentDueDate (used as billCycleCode)
- /rest/account/services → serviceId + serviceType
- /rest/usage → 13-month billing history (not /rest/usage/detail/month)

The usage endpoint requires serviceId, billCycleCode (paymentDueDate),
and the full serviceType string from the services endpoint.

Sensors updated to match the actual data format:
- Monthly energy usage (billedConsumption kWh)
- Monthly energy cost (billedCharge USD)
- Yearly total usage and cost (sum of 13 months)
